 {"id": "bd-8sho", "title": "bd-1j4 subtask: Hard-parts docs/parity/support-matrix truth reconciliation", "description": "Background:
- Hard-parts completion claims require synchronized docs/support matrix/feature parity narratives.

Goal:
- Reconcile hard-parts implementation reality with user-facing claims and machine-generated reports.

Deliverables:
1) Hard-parts status truth table across README/FEATURE_PARITY/support_matrix/reality report.
2) Drift guard updates for hard-parts claim consistency.
3) Explicit “deferred vs implemented” rationale for each hard-parts subsystem.

Acceptance Criteria:
- No contradictory hard-parts claims across docs/artifacts.
- Drift gates catch future inconsistency automatically.

Verification & Logging:
- Drift guard tests and fixtures.
- Structured logs for reconciliation checks and mismatches.", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "task", "assignee": "RedMaple", "created_at": "2026-02-12T15:03:33.576518493Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-02-13T08:52:20.345066285Z", "closed_at": "2026-02-13T08:52:20.345031921Z", "close_reason": "Completed hard-parts truth table reconciliation across README/FEATURE_PARITY/support_matrix/reality_report with new drift guard script, harness test, and CI wiring.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["critique", "docs", "drift", "hard-parts", "verification"], "comments": [{"id": 1063, "issue_id": "bd-8sho", "author": "Dicklesworthstone", "text": "RedMaple claiming bd-8sho and starting hard-parts truth reconciliation across README/FEATURE_PARITY/support_matrix/reality_report.\n\nPlanned deliverables:\n1)  artifact with per-subsystem implemented vs deferred rationale.\n2)  drift guard script.\n3)  guard test.\n4) doc updates in  and  to include explicit hard-parts truth lines tied to the artifact.", "created_at": "2026-02-13T08:48:02Z"}, {"id": 1064, "issue_id": "bd-8sho", "author": "Dicklesworthstone", "text": "Progress update (RedMaple): completed hard-parts docs/parity/support/reality truth reconciliation with drift guard wiring.\n\nImplemented:\n- New canonical artifact: tests/conformance/hard_parts_truth_table.v1.json\n  - per-subsystem status + implemented/deferred scope rationale for startup/threading/resolver/nss/locale/iconv\n  - support_matrix expectations per subsystem\n  - reality_report snapshot binding + contradiction summary\n- New drift guard: scripts/check_hard_parts_truth.sh\n  - validates artifact schema, README + FEATURE_PARITY hard-parts truth lines, support_matrix expectations, and reality_report snapshot alignment\n  - emits structured JSON event with trace_id/mode/api_family/outcome/artifact refs\n- New harness test: crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/hard_parts_truth_test.rs\n  - artifact schema validation\n  - guard script execution gate\n- Docs reconciled:\n  - README.md hard-parts truth section added\n  - FEATURE_PARITY.md hard-parts truth section added\n- CI wiring:\n  - scripts/ci.sh now runs scripts/check_hard_parts_truth.sh\n\nVerification executed (PASS):\n- scripts/check_hard_parts_truth.sh\n- CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/cargo-target-codex-harness cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test hard_parts_truth_test\n- CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/cargo-target-codex-harness cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test parity_report_drift_test\n\nNote:\n- /tmp is full on this host; verification used CARGO_TARGET_DIR under /data/tmp for deterministic local runs.", "created_at": "2026-02-13T08:52:17Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-8zsp","title":"Smoke-fuzz proptests for inet IP parsers","description":"parse_ipv4, parse_ipv6, inet_pton, inet_aton in frankenlibc_core::inet are classic security-sensitive parsers. Add Archetype-1 smoke-fuzz proptests: (a) never-panics on arbitrary bytes, (b) output bounded, (c) format-roundtrip for inputs that parse successfully (parse(format(x)) == x). Scope: crates/frankenlibc-core/src/inet/mod.rs #[cfg(test)] module.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T20:29:16.512627925Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T20:31:46.178725431Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T20:31:46.176686404Z","close_reason":"Added 8 inet smoke-fuzz proptests in commit 0cf34de: parse_ipv4 (never-panics, structured-alphabet, round-trip), parse_ipv6 (never-panics, structured-alphabet, round-trip), inet_pton (af-cube), inet_aton (legacy). ~4100 case evaluations per run, no panics found on first sweep. Covers historical CVE surface for dotted-quad arithmetic and IPv6 '::' expansion.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-95tq","title":"resolv: gai_cancel and gai_suspend empty requests should return EAI_ALLDONE","description":"Concrete regression: host glibc returns EAI_ALLDONE with errno unchanged for synchronous-degenerate async DNS calls like gai_cancel(NULL), gai_cancel(&zeroed_gaicb), gai_suspend(NULL, 0, timeout), and gai_suspend([NULL], 1, timeout). FrankenLibC currently returns EAI_SYSTEM with ENOSYS. Restore host-parity degenerate semantics in unistd_abi.rs and lock them with focused ABI tests.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T06:14:54.765525235Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T06:16:32.621315051Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-95tq","title":"resolv: gai_cancel and gai_suspend empty requests should return EAI_ALLDONE","description":"Concrete regression: host glibc returns EAI_ALLDONE with errno unchanged for synchronous-degenerate async DNS calls like gai_cancel(NULL), gai_cancel(&zeroed_gaicb), gai_suspend(NULL, 0, timeout), and gai_suspend([NULL], 1, timeout). FrankenLibC currently returns EAI_SYSTEM with ENOSYS. Restore host-parity degenerate semantics in unistd_abi.rs and lock them with focused ABI tests.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T06:14:54.765525235Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T06:19:52.577129952Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T06:19:52.574907623Z","close_reason":"Restored host-parity EAI_ALLDONE semantics for synchronous-degenerate gai_cancel/gai_suspend inputs and verified locally plus via rch.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-95zf","title":"AI_ADDRCONFIG review: null-node IPv6-only and dual-stack coverage","description":"Review of cod's bd-4qqu fix (commit 8996ad1 hoisting AI_ADDRCONFIG filter out of Some(text) branch) found 2 coverage gaps in the null-node test set: (1) IPv6-only interface state — the null-node IPv4 filter case is tested but the symmetric IPv6-only case is not, so an asymmetric filter bug would escape. (2) dual-stack interface state with null-node — no test verifies both AF_INET and AF_INET6 nodes are preserved when both families have non-loopback routes. Scope: crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/resolv_abi_test.rs additions only, no code changes.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T19:37:35.826818907Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T19:38:54.778398472Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T19:38:54.776390943Z","close_reason":"Added 2 complementary null-node AI_ADDRCONFIG tests in commit b935235: IPv6-only filter symmetry + dual-stack preservation. Suite now covers all 4 quadrants of the {IPv4,IPv6} x {filter,keep} matrix for null-node under AI_ADDRCONFIG.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-96nv","title":"Conformance: direct execute string_strtok fixture cases","description":"Add direct frankenlibc_fixture_exec coverage for string_strtok.json cases in the harness alongside existing isolated harness coverage.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SilverHarbor","created_at":"2026-04-18T07:48:45.060551994Z","created_by":"SilverHarbor","updated_at":"2026-04-18T07:54:40.353901880Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T07:54:40.352014970Z","close_reason":"Added direct execute_fixture_case coverage for string_strtok fixtures and verified with rch test/check/clippy.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","string","testing"]}
 {"id":"bd-9chy.10","title":"stdio: export _IO_2_1_stdin_/stdout_/stderr_ aliases bound to NativeFile storage","description":"## Background

Plan v4 §3 (final paragraph). Some statically-linked-against-libc binaries resolve _IO_2_1_stdin_, _IO_2_1_stdout_, _IO_2_1_stderr_ directly via dlsym or link-time resolution, bypassing the public stdin/stdout/stderr aliases. Without exporting these names from FrankenLibC, the binary sees two distinct streams (one native FrankenLibC, one host glibc) and the program misbehaves subtly: partial flushes, lost output, double-close.

## Depends on

bd-9chy.1 (NativeFile layout)

## What to do

1. Add the three symbol names to crates/frankenlibc-abi/version_scripts/libc.map under the GLIBC_2.2.5 version tag.
2. In stdio_abi.rs (or io_internal_abi.rs), declare the underscored names as #[no_mangle] static aliases pointing to the *same* NativeFile storage as the public stdin/stdout/stderr globals. Do NOT allocate a second set of NativeFile objects — they must be the same object so a flush via stdin reaches the same buffer as a flush via _IO_2_1_stdin_.
3. Verify the alias by building the .so and inspecting nm: nm -D target/release/libfrankenlibc_abi.so | grep _IO_2_1 must show the three symbols at the same address as stdin/stdout/stderr.
4. Add an integration test: build a small C program that takes both stdin and _IO_2_1_stdin_, asserts they are equal, and runs it under LD_PRELOAD.

## Done

- libc.map exports the three aliases
- nm verification passes
- Integration test passes
- The aliases survive a release build with strip+lto","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:28:41.061649453Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T00:24:31.706206591Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["foundation","stdio","version-script"]}
